sacco.gui

Class STextArea

  • java.lang.Object
    • sacco.gui.STextArea
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.awt.event.KeyListener, java.util.EventListener, SComponent

    public class STextArea
extends java.lang.Object
implements SComponent, java.awt.event.KeyListener

      Appends (adds to the end of the text) the parameter String.

      Determines whether or not the horizontal scroll should be added

      Modifies how the text area handles text wrapping.

      Sets the text of the Text Area to the given parameter

    • Method Detail

      • append

        public void append(java.lang.String text)
        Appends (adds to the end of the text) the parameter String. This is not followed by a new line
        Parameters:
        text - The text to append

      • appendln

        public void appendln(java.lang.String text)
        Appends (adds to the end of the text) the parameter String. This is followed by a new line
        Parameters:
        text - The text to append

      • getText

        public java.lang.String getText()

      • setVerticalScroll

        public void setVerticalScroll(boolean b)
        Determines whether or not the vertical scroll should be added
        Parameters:
        b - - true will always show the scrollbar. false will never

      • setHorizontalScroll

        public void setHorizontalScroll(boolean b)
        Determines whether or not the horizontal scroll should be added
        Parameters:
        b - - true will always show the scrollbar. false will never

      • getSelectedText

        public java.lang.String getSelectedText()
        returns the text that is currently highlighted in the window
        Returns:
        the text

      • setText

        public void setText(java.lang.String text)
        Sets the text of the Text Area to the given parameter
        Parameters:
        text -

      • setEditable

        public void setEditable(boolean b)
        Use this method to set the text area to be editable or not
        Parameters:
        b - true sets it to editable. false sets it to not editable

      • setLineWrap

        public void setLineWrap(boolean b)
        Modifies how the text area handles text wrapping.
        Parameters:
        b - true signifies that the text should wrap. false signifies that it should not.
